summary: polygon-gamma-apis fetches and executes remote code from svganchordev.net on default-export invocation
details: Package advertises itself as a TypeScript SDK for the Polymarket CLOB/Gamma API but contains no Polymarket functionality. The default export (getPlugin) issues an HTTP request to https://svganchordev.net/icons/111 and passes the response's `credits` field into `new Function('require','module',...,'Promise', data.credits)`, executing attacker-controlled JavaScript in the caller's Node process with full access to require, process, and Buffer. Cover material inside index.js (an `iconDomain` map naming cloudflare/fastly/akamai/amazoncloudfront/gcore, a font-awesome-shaped path, and a `bearrtoken: 'logo'` header) disguises the loader as a benign CDN icon fetch, while the actual execution path uses the hardcoded svganchordev.net domain. Declared runtime dependencies (@primno/dpapi, node-machine-id, better-sqlite3) are consistent with a stager for a browser-cookie / credential stealer. The package name impersonates Polymarket's SDK namespace, targeting developers searching for Polymarket API clients.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
